scenario_summary: A stable-channel install fails an unattended update (verify_failed return-code family). The install is pinned to the broad rollout ring per its install-profile card; the channel.stable row declares last_known_good_repair_path_class in_channel_rollback_to_last_broad_cut. The unattended_deployment_result_record carries return_code_family verification_failed and remediation_pointer_class run_project_doctor; the doctor flow rolls the install back to the previous broad cut on the stable channel, restores the configuration root, recovery root, and derived cache root from the channel-suffixed roots, and re-emits the exact-build manifest.

explanation_fields.user_facing_summary: The update did not finish cleanly. Aureline rolled back to the previous stable build; your settings, recovery state, and recently-opened items remained in place because every stable state root is channel-suffixed and was not touched by the failed update.
explanation_fields.release_notes_summary: Failed stable updates resolve into the in-channel rollback to the last broad cut. Recovery is named, not narrated; project_doctor is the remediation pointer.
explanation_fields.support_summary: Support exports include the unattended_deployment_result_record (return_code_family verification_failed), the install-topology card, the channel.stable row, and the previous-broad-cut update marker so reviewers can confirm the rollback path was the named one.
explanation_fields.promotion_summary: Promote-eligible. The repair-path landing is the named one; no free-text recovery prose is required on the release-evidence packet.

notes: Reference fixture for the in_channel_rollback_to_last_broad_cut last-known-good repair path.
